sweep_evidence.note: Fix delta classified as: contract change (eligibility reason routing, two new record-dispatch refusals), new guards, and coupled prose. Ran 2.3.0 relocation (the re-scoped step-3-6 paragraphs vs their module pins — both pinned literals restored verbatim after the first rewording broke them, caught by the module), 2.3.0a peer-checkpoint (the unestablished-by-construction claim across the three fallback references), 2.3.0b enum reconciliation (_STEERING_SUMMARY_REASONS added beside _STEERING_SUMMARY; _DEGRADED_REASONS mirrored into the skill's --reason enumeration), 2.3.0c operand trace (the refusal's new comparand is _clean_identity, the same operation the grant consumes — one producer, two consumers), 2.3.4a self-authored-claim (every docstring the fix falsified was rewritten: triggers 'exactly the three', eligibility 'require the opposite', _validate 'indexes every one of these keys', determinism 'reproduces exactly'), 2.3.6 error handling (the generator loader now fails closed on any module-body exception).

phase3_findings.description: step-3-6-audit's preserved information-diet / out-of-bounds / carriage paragraphs still direct a non-bare dispatch prompt, which would make the auditor answer extra-dispatch-content: yes on every normal run
evaluate_triggers docstring claims exactly three reason-bearing arms and that T2's behavior on a clean FILE round is unchanged; the same diff adds a fourth arm that changes exactly that
steering-unestablished masks unaudited-revision, and the justifying docstring/comment is false against HEAD
the 'refuses the pair half-given' claim is false in the reverse direction
the generation-failure recipe orders record-degraded before the record-dispatch that creates the round, omits --round, and overloads the reserved degraded flag
_default_template_path is reached across the module boundary outside the fail-closed try, so an AttributeError escapes as a traceback and aborts record-return before save_state
the attempt carries two draft-path facts that must agree and nothing makes them
docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md still states the state schema_version is 2
the event-ordering ground is unreachable under the steering gate and fallback-audit-dispatch-arms claims the arm still carries it
the generated di instructions assert to the auditor, as fact, the one property the design says it cannot prove
_validate comment claims record-return indexes every key of the instructions record
the new _validate branches carry no malformed-state rows, including the forged-pair check
the template_path regeneration input and the _assemble fail-closed arm are uncovered
steering_reason has no closed answer set while steering does
instructions['digest'] is write-only state with a strict invariant and no consumer
the T2 offer fires on structurally-unestablishable arms where a re-dispatch cannot help
byte-exactness of the instruction-file write is not specified
renderer module docstring splice artifact and draft_title coupled-mirror overstatement

fix_decisions.claim_text: instructions['digest'] is write-only state with a strict invariant and no consumer
fix_decisions.evidence: The field is a deliberate dispatch-time diagnostic, not dead state: it is what the `instructions_digest=` line prints for the orchestrator's own record, and the comparand is deliberately the fresh regeneration (regenerate_instructions_digest's docstring states why comparing the recorded digest would be self-consistent and useless). Wiring it into record-return as an extra comparison would detect a different property (the file mutating between dispatch and return) that the regeneration comparison already subsumes — a mutated file fails the canonical compare too. Fixed the _validate comment that mis-stated why it is validated instead.

parking_evidence.basis: the prescription rests on the field being unused, but its use is the printed dispatch-time diagnostic and the regeneration comparison already covers the shape it would detect
parking_evidence.failing_input: an instruction file mutated between dispatch and return

fix_decisions.claim_text: the T2 offer fires on structurally-unestablishable arms where a re-dispatch cannot help
fix_decisions.evidence: A re-dispatch on an embed/inline round CAN change the outcome: those arms are entered because the canonical draft-file write failed, and the accepted re-audit re-runs query-arm, which returns arm=file once the write succeeds — so the offer is actionable, not a loop. Suppressing it would also remove the only user-facing disclosure on exactly the arms whose independence is weakest.

parking_evidence.basis: the claim assumes the arm is fixed for the run; query-arm re-decides it per round
parking_evidence.failing_input: an embed-arm round whose next dispatch finds the canonical write succeeding

fix_decisions.claim_text: byte-exactness of the instruction-file write is not specified
fix_decisions.evidence: Real and worth stating, but the prose already says 'write the renderer's stdout verbatim' and the failure it guards (a trailing-newline normalization) is disclosed by the mismatch reason token rather than silently swallowed. Adding a redirect form to the fence would also change the create-issue default-path word budget again in the same iteration; left for the shadow/next pass to weigh against the ceiling.

completeness_critic.population: the steering (state, reason) pairs the round record may carry
completeness_critic.independent_signal: the literal return tuples of steering_state, enumerated by regex over its own body (not the mapping the audit reads)
completeness_critic.result: complete with respect to that signal — all 8 producer pairs are covered, zero uncovered

verification_evidence.command: lib/test/run.sh (via a SIG_DFL launcher) + ruff + shellcheck
